message.content:
RENT AGREEMENT

This Agreement is executed at Kharar on this 1st May 2023.

a) Gurkanwal Singh S/o Hakim Singh R/o House No. 2263, Phase 7, Mohali. (Hereinafter called to as the land lord and 1st party of the rent deed) which terms shall where the context so admits, his heirs, executors, administers of the one part of this deed.

AND

b) Vivek Walia (Aadhar No. 2183 4533 8406) S/o Raj Kumar R/o Mandi Himachal Pradesh. herein after called to as the tenant and the 2nd party of the deed which terms shall also where the context so admits his heirs, executors, administrators, assignees and legal representative etc. of the other part of this deed.

WHEREAS the above said first party is the owner of SCO No 02, Basement Cabin 1, New Sunny Enclave, Sector 125, Kharar, SAS Nagar (Mohali) Punjab. and the above said second party has contacted the 1st Party to take on rent in the above said shop and the 1st party has agreed to let out the said premises to the above said tenant on the following terms and conditions:-

1. That the monthly rent money of the said premises has been fixed Rs. 5,000 (Five Thousand Rupees) per month payable in Advance.
2. That the tenant has paid advance Rs. 5,000 (Five Thousand Rupees) per on account of security to above said landlord. The amount of Security shall carry no interest and the Security amount will be refunded to the Tenant on vacation/expiry of the agreement/rented premises after adjusting the electricity bills/damage if any found due. Security will not adjust in the rent. If tenant vacate above said rented premises before six months that case security will not be refundable.
3. That the tenancy has begun with commenced w.e.f. 01-05-2023 to 31-03-2024 shall be for Eleven month in the first instance.
4. That the premises shall be used for Commercial only and no other purpose without the consent of the 1st party.
5. That the rent has to be paid in advance by 7th day of each English Calendar Month.
6. Tenant shall not sublet or part with the possession of the premises to anybody else.
7. That in case both the parties agree, and then the tenancy can be further extended and such extension shall be allowed with an increase of 10% in rent.
8. That the tenant shall pay the water electricity, (current meter/sub meter reading due ......... to the landlord/authority concerned during the tenancy period for the period under tenancy. If the connection is disconnected then all the fines etc. shall be deposit by the tenant to get the same connected again. Electricity bill if did not due date ten percent Fine. The tenant will be obliged to pay the full electricity bill on the date the electricity bill arrives, not half the bill.
9. That the tenant shall not make any additions or alternations in said premises without the written consent of the 1st party.

the year 2019-20 and if during the pendency of suit, defendants succeed in uprooting boundary wall, blocking the entry of plaintiff over suit land as well as causing sound and air pollution nuisance, then decree for mandatory injunction directing the defendants to restore the boundary wall to its original nature, remove blocking of passage and stop causing sound and air pollution nuisance as well as decree for possession and damages, may kindly be also passed against the defendants and in favour of plaintiff, in the interest of justice and law.

Dated: 12.6.2025

Place: Hamirpur. Through counsel Plaintiff

Sh. Ashan K. Kaushal Advocate

VERIFICATION:- I, Babli @ Babli Sharma plaintiff hereby verify that the contents from para No.1 to 9 of the plaint are true to the best of my knowledge and belief and nothing material has been concealed therein.

Verified on 12th June, 2025 at Hamirpur (HP). Plaintiff

upon suit land as mentioned above and since then same is
continued one.

7. That the parties to the suit land and subject matter of the 
suit land is situated in Village Budhwin, P.O., Mauza &
Tehsil Galore, Distt. Hamirpur (HP), as per the jamabandi
for the year 2019-20, which is situated within the
territorial jurisdiction of the Hon'ble court.

8. That talvue of suit for purpose of court fee and jurisdiction
is 698/- over which court fees of Rs. 98/- is affixed.

9. That no similar suit on the same cause of action between
the same parties either pending or has been decided earlier
by any court of law.

It is therefore prayed that decree for 
permanent prohibitory injunction restraining the 
defendants, their family members, servants, agents and 
assignees from interfering in any manner what so ever, 
uprooting or demolishing boundary wall, forcibly 
blocking the entry of plaintiff in any manner as well as 
from creating sound and air pollution by generator set or 
machinery over suit the land comprised in khata No: 133, 
Khatauni No. 177, Khasra No. 567, measuring 00-02-54 
hectare & Khasra No. 568 measuring 00-01-17 hectare 
situated in village Budhwin, P.O., Mauza & Tehsil 
Galore, Distt. Hamirpur (HP), as per the jamabandi for

parking vehicle in front of her gate as well as a Kalandra has also been processed for harassing, terrorizing, abusing and threatening the plaintiff by the defendants.

4. That however when the plaintiff asked defendants to stop such blocking of her entry to her suit land/ house, then they openly threatened the plaintiff with dire consequences and even earlier also she has been tried to hit by vehicle. Defendants further tried to uproot boundary wall forcibly. They also not adhered to the request of the plaintiff, who as mentioned above is alone lady and not in a position to thwart such actions of defendants physically. Defendants now on regular basis threatening the plaintiff for blocking her entry to the suit land/ house and blocked such entry as mentioned above.

5. That plaintiff is alone lady and her family resides out of country due to exigency of their work, whereas she has taken demarcation of her land, which was accepted by adjoining owners including defendants. But now plaintiff is not in a position to resist such claim of defendants on each and every occasion physically, hence present suit.

6. That the cause of action accrued to the plaintiff on in first week of June, 2025 when the defendants forcibly entered over the suit land without any authority and started threatening the plaintiff, whereby they abused and attempted to cause assault on her and further forcibly tried to uproot the boundary wall and boundary marks

1.	
Khasra No. 568 measuring 00-01-17 hectare situated in 
village Budhwin, P.O., Mauza & Tehsil Galore, Distt. 
Hamirpur (HP), as per the jamabandi for the year 2019- 
20, is owned and possessed by plaintiff. (A duly attested 
affidavit and Jamabandi year 2019-20 are appended 
herewith). 

2. That the defendants are stranger to the suit land, who 
have no right, title or interest over the suit land although 
they are adjoining owner to the suit land where running 
petrol pimp under the name and style Nyara. 

3. That defendants being head strong person, having no 
respect for the law of the land and with malafide intention 
to cause irreparable loss and damage to the plaintiff, who 
is alone lady and suffering from certain ailments, started 
claiming boundary wall of plaintiff as their own and 
trying to demolish and uproot the same and also in 
number of ways blocking entry of the plaintiff over suit 
land by parking their vehicles over such entry over suit 
land and openly proclaiming such land of plaintiff as their 
own. Even now they have installed generator set and 
other heavy machinery just adjacent to the boundary wall 
of plaintiff and all the air and sound pollution nuisance 
has been directed towards the house of plaintiff. Although 
for such nuisance separate legal proceedings have been 
launched and even an F.I.R. has been lodged against 
defendant No.1 for blocking the passage of plaintiff by

scientific research on optimal reclamation systems and development of new irrigation
technologies. The importance of their work was the implementation of the Cabinet
ministers’ decree about reducing the volume of production of raw cotton, and to
reduce the area of cotton fields, in the certain areas the need to expand crop fields in
cereal grains, fertilization of vegetable crops. An investigations on improving the
irrigation of the tailing dumps have been conducted by the Institute of Nguen III Xe
Kuang, Garsia Soto Rafael, Le Zuan, Kake Maju, Abdul Kadir Abbos, A.Kuchkarov,
Ahmad Hasan. These studies cover the issues of uniform water flow along the edges,
weeds control, pipes and rainwater irrigation, abatement and permanent water outages.
In these studies it has been given sufficient attention to the combination of water
regiment and herbicides.

scientific research on optimal reclamation systems and development of new irrigation

technologies. The importance of their work was the implementation of the Cabinet
ministers' decree about reducing the volume of production of raw cotton, and to
reduce the area of cotton fields, in the certain areas the need to expand crop fields in
cereal grains, fertilization of vegetable crops. An investigations on improving the
irrigation of the tailing dumps have been conducted by the Institute of Nguen III Xe
Kuang, Garsia Soto Rafael, Le Zuan, Kake Maju, Abdul Kadir Abbos, A.Kuchkarov,
Ahmad Hasan. These studies cover the issues of uniform water flow along the edges,
weeds control, pipes and rainwater irrigation, abatement and permanent water outages.
In these studies it has been given sufficient attention to the combination of water
regiment and herbicides.

ANALYSIS AND RESULTS
Specialization in agriculture is a complex versatile process; it covers not only
agriculture but other areas that are linked to it as well. The implementation of a specialization process as stated above are required a consideration the past few factors, which the main criteria would be providing the highest utility level and the competitiveness of the product.

In the conditions of market economy the factors influencing to specialization can be divided into two groups: natural and economic factors (Figure 1)
